hero.h1: Every Project in Romania Looks Investment-Ready.
hero.subtitle: Technical Validation Shows a Different Picture.
hero.heroBody: A project's investment value lies not in its documents — but in its grid connectivity and the true integrity of its permit chain.

b2.paragraph1: AFA Energy is an expert company based in Istanbul and Bucharest, providing technical assessment and advisory services in the Romanian and Turkish renewable energy markets.
b2.paragraph2: Through the ATR Matrix™ methodology, we select only projects with solid technical fundamentals from those we examine and connect them with institutional investors. The bond between developer and investor is the product of our selection discipline.

statusCards.name: Proceed
statusCards.desc: Becomes visible on the investor side

statusCards.name: Conditional
statusCards.desc: Can be evaluated with defined risk conditions

statusCards.name: Blocking Findings
statusCards.desc: Does not become visible on the investor side

b2.footnote: AFA preliminary technical assessment covers analysis of ATR documents and examination of grid connection conditions.
b2.pullLine2: on a project with verified technical reality

b3.h2: Every Project Is Examined on Four Axes.
b3.intro: AFA subjects every project it examines to the same methodological discipline before the investment decision. The project phase may change, but the framework does not.
b3.ctaBandH2: Evaluate your projects with ATR Matrix™
b3.ctaBandBody: Identify technical risks at an early stage with our 4-axis analysis methodology and strengthen your investment decisions.

cards.title: Connection Applicability
cards.body: The actual applicability of ATR documents and connection conditions is examined. Gaps between documentation and field reality are identified.

cards.title: Grid Capacity and Production Curtailment Risk
cards.body: Regional grid load, existing capacity, and production curtailment probability are assessed. Curtailment risk is made visible before the investment decision.

cards.title: Connection Cost and Grid Investment
cards.body: Connection fees, additional grid works, and possible reinforcement costs are examined. Hidden CAPEX risks that may arise post-ATR are made visible at an early stage.

cards.title: Permit Chain and Schedule Risk
cards.body: The project's permit chain and regulatory processes are assessed for chronological consistency. The impact of missing permits and procedural gaps on the investment schedule is analyzed.

atrBand.line2: on a project with verified technical reality

cards.title: ATR Matrix™ — Grid Connection Analysis
cards.description: The financial impact of connection conditions is made visible at an early stage; uncertainty is transformed into an actionable assessment basis for investment decisions.

cards.title: Investment Committee-Ready Deliverables
cards.description: Every finding is defined, classified, and structured to strengthen the decision basis. Deliverables are prepared in formats aligned with investment committee review standards.

cards.title: Turkey–Romania Bridge
cards.description: Different grid structures and regulatory frameworks are addressed together within a comparable analytical framework, enabling consistent cross-market comparison.

cards.title: International Financing Alignment
cards.description: The analytical and reporting structure is designed to align with technical due diligence expectations grounded in a bankability perspective.

cards.title: Selectivity Is a Value Discipline
cards.description: Projects with high uncertainty are filtered before reaching investor visibility; the presented pool is maintained at a consistent maturity level.

romania.title: Romania
romania.breakingPoint: An ATR document grants theoretical connection rights; however, actual grid capacity and curtailment risk may paint a different picture than the document suggests.
romania.boardTranslation: Uncertainty bands form around revenue visibility, schedule realism, and bankability — directly affecting which risks can be assumed and under what conditions.

turkey.title: Turkey
turkey.breakingPoint: The regulatory framework appears broadly stable, but implementation can diverge at the electricity distribution company level — divergences that become visible in connection realities.
turkey.boardTranslation: Implementation variability may widen the uncertainty band around project timelines, permit chains, and contractual risk allocation, affecting cash flow reliability.

b5.pullQuoteMain: The same project headline generates different decision discussions in two markets due to different sources of uncertainty. Reading market dynamics within a comparable framework gives the investment committee the tools to distinguish these differences at an early stage.
b5.pullQuoteSub: This Romania-centred analytical framework lays the groundwork for an expanding scope targeting emerging renewable energy markets across Central and Eastern Europe.

b6b7.subhead1: The right project. The right investor.
b6b7.description: For investors seeking projects with verified technical reality in Romania — or for developers looking to initiate their project's assessment process — this is the right place.
b6b7.disclaimer: AFA's work covers preliminary technical assessment and process coordination. Independent legal due diligence and any required technical verifications should be conducted separately prior to final project-specific decisions.
